verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 encodes TGN46/TGN51, a type I integral membrane protein that resides constitutively in the trans-Golgi network and cycles between early endosomes and TGN. Its topology places the lumenal/extracellular domain inside the TGN/endosomal lumen, not the extracellular face of the plasma membrane. Pre-no checklist: (1) No cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets this protein body on the outer leaflet. (2) Not an ectodomain-shedding TM precursor with documented surface residency. (3) No wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the plasma membrane outer leaflet. (4) No documented PM minority pool — it cycles between TGN and early endosomes, not the cell surface. (5) No cancer/cell-death/activation/developmental outer-leaflet display documented for this protein specifically. It is a canonical endomembrane-resident TM protein with no evidence of outer-leaflet accessibility.
